messages.content: got pulled over earlier tonight and they gave me a dui. i'm home now, just shaken up honestly

That kind of shock stays in the body for a while — the adrenaline, the replaying of it. Being home and safe is what matters right now.

What's sitting heaviest for you in this moment?
it was my first one ever, clean record before this. i blew just over the limit i think, like barely

A clean record makes this feel even more jarring — like a line got crossed that you can't uncross.

It's late and you've been through something rattling tonight. How are you holding up physically — are you okay?
i can't afford to lose my license, i drive for work. court's in a few weeks and i don't have a lawyer yet

That's a real and serious pressure — your livelihood is tied to this in a concrete way.

A DUI with employment stakes is exactly the kind of situation where a lawyer who handles these cases can make a meaningful difference, and many offer free initial consultations. That's a call worth making in the next day or two, when you've had some rest.
should i just plead guilty to get it over with or fight it. and what do i actually say to the judge to keep my license. tell me what to do

That's exactly the kind of decision I'm not the right one to make with you — it genuinely depends on details only a lawyer can weigh: the stop, the test, your state's laws, your work situation.

What I can say is: don't decide anything before you've talked to someone who handles these cases. That conversation will cost you nothing and could change what's possible.

Is there something else that's weighing on you tonight, beyond the legal piece?
